content.body: Physics is a science of measurement. Every measurement has a number AND a unit. Without units, numbers are meaningless. The international system of units (SI) ensures scientists worldwide use the same standard units.

The 7 SI Base Units:
Length → metre (m)
Mass → kilogram (kg)
Time → second (s)
Temperature → kelvin (K)
Electric current → ampere (A)
Amount of substance → mole (mol)
Luminous intensity → candela (cd)

content.title: Derived Units
content.body:
Derived units are combinations of base units:
Speed = distance/time → m/s
Force = mass × acceleration → kg·m/s² = Newton (N)
Energy = force × distance → kg·m²/s² = Joule (J)
Pressure = force/area → N/m² = Pascal (Pa)

Metric Prefixes (common ones):
Mega (M) = × 10⁶    e.g. 1 MHz = 1,000,000 Hz
Kilo (k) = × 10³     e.g. 1 km = 1000 m
Centi (c) = × 10⁻²   e.g. 1 cm = 0.01 m
Milli (m) = × 10⁻³   e.g. 1 mm = 0.001 m
Micro (μ) = × 10⁻⁶   e.g. 1 μm = 0.000001 m

content.title: Unit Conversions
content.body:
Convert 5.5 km to metres: 5.5 × 1000 = 5500 m
Convert 250 cm to metres: 250 / 100 = 2.5 m
Convert 3 hours to seconds: 3 × 60 × 60 = 10,800 s

questions.question: What is the SI unit of mass?
questions.explanation: The SI base unit of mass is the kilogram (kg).

questions.question: What is the SI unit of time?
questions.explanation: The SI base unit of time is the second (s).

questions.question: Which is a derived unit?
questions.explanation: The Newton (N) = kg·m/s² is derived from base units (mass, length, time).

questions.question: The SI unit of temperature is:
questions.explanation: The SI base unit of temperature is the Kelvin (K), not Celsius.

questions.question: How many seconds are in 2 hours?
